title: Hemionitis subpaleacea
intro: Hemionitis subpaleacea is a species of fern native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, protecting against cell damage, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Hemionitis subpaleacea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hemionitis subpaleacea is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
taxonomy: Hemionitis subpaleacea belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which includes many species of ferns.
